name: Michigan State University Extension Services
description_short: Michigan State University Extension's free financial and housing education program offering certified counselors, webinars, and workshops for money management and homeownership.
description_long:
Michigan State University Extension Services operates MI Money Health, a statewide financial education initiative funded through the public university system. The program was established to provide unbiased, accessible financial literacy and housing counseling to Michigan residents regardless of income level. MI Money Health serves as a bridge between academic research and practical community financial education.

MI Money Health offers a comprehensive suite of free services including certified housing and money management counseling, online and in-person financial education classes, foreclosure prevention assistance, homeownership seminars, self-paced learning modules, and specialized workshops. Their content includes articles, videos, publications, and events covering topics like the home buying process, rent preparation (Rent Smart program), estate planning sensitivity ("Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Pie Plate?"), and consumer protection awareness. The organization provides both one-time presentations and ongoing educational programs adapted to emerging community financial issues.

What distinguishes MI Money Health is its university affiliation, certified counselor staff, and explicit commitment to serving diverse audiences regardless of financial status. As an Extension program, it operates without profit motive and publishes documented program impact reports annually. The organization combines traditional housing counseling with modern delivery methods including Zoom webinars and mobile apps (Goosechase), and specifically targets youth aged 13-19 alongside adult learners.

The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are restricted to Michigan residents. While the website demonstrates active programming and impact measurement, specific counselor response times, wait periods, and the depth of one-on-one foreclosure assistance availability are not detailed on the website. For consumers seeking intensive debt negotiation or credit repair, this is an educational resource rather than a transactional service.
pros: Certified housing and money management counselors providing unbiased education
Completely free services with no commercial interest or profit motive
Multiple delivery formats: in-person classes, virtual webinars, self-paced online learning, and mobile app games
Published annual impact reports documenting outcomes for 2020 and 2021
Serves diverse audiences explicitly regardless of financial status or background
Specialized programs addressing specific life events: foreclosure, first-time homebuying, rental preparation, estate planning
cons: Services limited to Michigan residents only
No clearly stated response times or availability for one-on-one counseling
Website does not specify counselor qualifications details or certifying bodies beyond mention of 'certified'
Limited information about wait times or scheduling capacity for in-person services
Educational focus means no debt negotiation or active account management services provided
best_for: Michigan residents seeking free homeownership education before purchasing a first home
Homeowners facing foreclosure looking for prevention resources and counseling
Young adults (13-19) and families wanting foundational money management education
Michigan renters preparing for the rental process or improving tenant conduct
diagnosis: MI Money Health is best for Michigan residents seeking free, unbiased financial education and counseling from certified professionals, particularly those preparing for homeownership, facing foreclosure, or wanting foundational money management skills. The critical caveat is that this is an educational resource, not a transactional service—it provides knowledge and guidance but does not actively negotiate debts, repair credit reports, or manage accounts on behalf of consumers.
services: Self-paced financial learning modules (articles, videos, publications)
Rent Smart program for rental financial preparation
Consumer fraud and scam awareness training
Smart Money Missions youth financial game (Goosechase app)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 446 W Circle Dr, East Lansing, MI 48824
